sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Angela, reports being "pretty well" today.
She has a history of kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode, and a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes.
She reports that her pain from the kidney stones has resolved, and she is no longer noticing blood in her urine.
She takes metformin 500mg daily for diabetes and norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension.
She has been having difficulty controlling her diet, consuming fast food and sweets frequently, and has not been checking her blood sugars regularly.
Objective:
Vital signs: blood pressure 150/90, but she has had readings of 130-140/60-70 at home.
The physical exam was notable for a grade 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur, which has not worsened since her last visit.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, which appear to have passed this time, but she needs further evaluation and management for her recurrent kidney stones.
She also has hypertension and diabetes, which are being managed with medication and lifestyle modifications.
Problem list: Recurrent kidney stones; hypertension; diabetes; insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed metformin 500mg daily for diabetes, and norvasc 2.5mg daily remains unchanged.
I gave her a referral to urology to evaluate and manage her recurrent kidney stones.
I also gave her a consult to a nutritionist to help her with diet modifications.
I advised her to take 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia, and to practice proper sleep hygiene.
I encouraged her to take her blood sugars before every meal to gauge her blood sugar control.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in three months for follow-up.
